title: How to Heal Bleeding Gums with 7 Best Herbal Tinctures

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides help to reduce bleeding gums by stopping the growth of bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection, thus promoting healthy gums and preventing bleeding.

Cichoric acid is good for bleeding gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth in the gums, thereby promoting healthy gum tissue and stopping bleeding.

Polyphenols are good for bleeding gums because they have strong antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which can help to stop the bleeding and promote healing in the gums.
Caffeic acid is good for bleeding gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that can cause gum infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a natural remedy that may help soothe bleeding gums. This is because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. When you have bleeding gums, it's usually because of inflammation caused by a bacterial infection or poor oral hygiene. Echinacea purpurea tincture contains flavonoids and alkylamides that can help fight off these infections and reduce inflammation, making it a potential natural solution to calm bleeding gums. Additionally, the tincture's antiseptic properties can help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, promoting a healthy environment for your gums to heal.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture for bleeding gums, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, prepare the Ginkgo biloba leaves by crushing them into small pieces and then mixing them with a carrier solvent like vodka in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is to use one part of the crushed Ginkgo biloba leaves to five parts of the solvent. For example, if you are using 100 grams of Ginkgo biloba leaves, you would mix them with 500 grams of vodka. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the solvent, then seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the active ingredients from the Ginkgo biloba leaves into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and store the Ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark glass bottle, away from children and pets. This tincture can be taken orally, 2-3 times a day, by mixing a few drops of it with water, to help reduce bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for bleeding gums

Tannins are good for bleeding gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing infections.

Ursolic acid is good for bleeding gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and fight infection in the gum tissues, promoting healthy healing and preventing further irritation.
Luteolin is good for bleeding gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and prevent bleeding by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help with oral health issues. The tincture made from this herb is good for bleeding gums because it contains compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that can cause infection and bleeding in the gums. The tincture also contains antioxidants that help to protect the gums from damage and promote healing. When applied to the gums, the tincture can help to reduce bleeding and inflammation, and promote a healthy environment for the gums to heal. Additionally, the tincture has been shown to have astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. This makes it a useful natural remedy for people who experience bleeding gums due to various reasons, such as gum disease or poor oral hygiene.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with bleeding gums, first, you'll need Ginkgo biloba leaves and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Ginkgo biloba leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can aid in healing bleeding gums. To start, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely to prevent mold and bacteria growth. Then, chop the dried leaves into small pieces and mix them with the chosen solvent in a glass jar. The general ratio for a tincture is one part herb to four parts solvent. Fill the jar to the top and seal it tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent extract the herb's active compounds. After the extraction period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken orally with a few drops added to water or another liquid, usually 3-5 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Ginkgo biloba tincture, especially if you're taking medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tincture is good for bleeding gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain. The tincture also contains antioxidants that fight free radicals and promote healing. Calendula is a natural antiseptic that kills bacteria and other germs that can cause infection and worsen gum bleeding. When applied to the gums, it can help stop bleeding and promote the growth of new tissue. Its antimicrobial properties also prevent the spread of infection, allowing the gums to heal faster. Additionally, Calendula tincture has been traditionally used to treat mouth ulcers and sore throats, indicating its effectiveness in soothing and healing oral tissues. Regular use of Calendula tincture may help prevent gum bleeding and promote overall oral health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for bleeding gums, you'll need a few basic tools and some patience. First, collect fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and make sure they are free from contaminants. Then, fill a clean glass jar with 1 cup of the leaves, leaving about 1 inch of space at the top. Next, pour 2 cups of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process allows the active compounds from the Ginkgo biloba leaves to infuse into the alcohol. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. You can now use your Ginkgo biloba tincture as directed, typically by taking 20-30 drops in water 2-3 times a day. Keep in mind that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy that can help with bleeding gums. This can help to stop bleeding and reduce discomfort in the gums. The antibacterial properties of Melaleuca alternifolia tincture can also help to prevent the spread of infection, which can help to promote healing in the gums. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia tincture may also help to reduce the risk of gum disease and promote overall gum health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture for bleeding gums, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, vodka, and a glass jar with a lid. Start by gathering one part of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and adding it to a clean glass jar. Then, add three parts of vodka to the jar, making sure that the leaves are completely covered by the liquid. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the vodka extract the healing properties from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This liquid is your Ginkgo biloba tincture, which you can use to treat bleeding gums by applying a few drops to the affected area with a cotton swab, 2-3 times a day. Always dilute the tincture with water before applying it to the gums, as it is quite strong.

Eucalyptus globulus tincture can help to calm the gums and reduce bleeding by applying a few drops to a cotton swab and gently rubbing it on the affected area. This can help to reduce discomfort and promote healing. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tincture can help to freshen breath and reduce bad odors associated with gum disease. It is also a good alternative for people who are allergic to traditional medications or prefer a natural approach to oral care.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with bleeding gums, you'll need some dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean coffee filter. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture for bleeding gums, you will need fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, vodka or another high-proof spirit, and a dark glass bottle for storage. Start by gathering or purchasing fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves, then clean and dry them thoroughly. Next, fill the glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried leaves. Pour vodka over the leaves, making sure that they are completely covered, and seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the leaves infuse into the vodka. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Use the Ginkgo biloba tincture as needed, mixing a few drops with water and swishing it around in your mouth before spitting it out. intro_desc: Bleeding gums can be a painful and embarrassing issue, but using herbal tinctures can help. Echinacea purpurea, also known as coneflower, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. It also has antimicrobial properties that can fight off bacteria that cause infections. Salvia officinalis, or sage, has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections and reduce inflammation. Both of these herbs can help fix the issue of bleeding gums by reducing pain and preventing infections.  Bleeding gums can be a sign of a bigger problem, like gum disease or poor oral hygiene. If left untreated, it can lead to tooth loss and other health problems. Fixing the issue of bleeding gums can improve your life by giving you confidence in your smile, and preventing more serious health problems. It can also improve your overall health by reducing inflammation and preventing infections. To make herbal tinctures work for you, start by consulting with a healthcare professional, then mix a few drops of the tinctures with water and swish it around your mouth before spitting it out. This can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in your gums.
supplementary_best: To remedy bleeding gums, a combination of Echinacea purpurea, Calendula officinalis, and Aloe barbadensis tinctures is effective. Echinacea purpurea helps to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that can cause gum bleeding. Its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the gums, making it an essential herb in this blend. Calendula officinalis is known for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing of the gums. It also reduces inflammation and swelling, providing relief from pain and discomfort. Aloe barbadensis is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the gums. Its gel-like texture also provides a protective barrier, preventing irritation and promoting faster healing. This combination of herbs works synergistically to tackle the root causes of bleeding gums, including inflammation, infection, and irritation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have bleeding gums, it's best to avoid certain herbal tinctures that may worsen the condition. One such herb is Willow bark (Salix alba), which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Taking it can increase bleeding due to its blood-thinning properties, making it harder for your gums to heal.  Another herb to avoid is Ginger (Zingiber officinale), which can thin the blood and increase bleeding. While ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that may help with gum inflammation, its blood-thinning effects can counteract this benefit and worsen bleeding gums.  St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is another herb to steer clear of if you have bleeding gums. It's known for its blood-thinning properties, which can increase bleeding and make it harder for your gums to heal.  Also, avoid using Tumeric (Curcuma longa) tincture if you have bleeding gums. Turmeric contains curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and blood-thinning properties. While these properties can help with gum inflammation, they can also worsen bleeding gums by thinning the blood.  Finally, avoid using Garlic (Allium sativum) tincture if you have bleeding gums. Garlic has blood-thinning properties that can increase bleeding and make it harder for your gums to heal.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bleeding gums can be a painful and uncomfortable issue. Besides herbal tinctures, there are other natural remedies that can help. One of these is oil pulling with coconut oil, which helps to reduce bacteria in the mouth and reduce inflammation. The antimicrobial properties of coconut oil help to fight off the bacteria that cause gum disease. Another remedy is saltwater rinse, which helps to reduce bacteria and inflammation in the gums. The antibacterial properties of salt help to kill the bacteria that cause bleeding gums. Additionally, aloe vera gel can be applied to the gums to help soothe and calm them. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Brushing your teeth with baking soda can also help to reduce bacteria and inflammation in the gums. The mild abrasive properties of baking soda help to remove plaque and bacteria from the teeth, which can help to prevent bleeding gums. These remedies can be used in combination with good oral hygiene practices, such as brushing and flossing regularly, to help prevent and treat bleeding gums.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with bleeding gums often experience other health issues. One common problem is bad breath, also known as halitosis. This is because the bacteria that cause gum disease can also spread to the mouth, leading to an unpleasant odor. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and eucalyptus can help with bad breath. Peppermint has antibacterial properties that reduce the growth of bacteria, while eucalyptus has a deodorizing effect that helps to freshen breath.  Another issue people with bleeding gums may face is swollen lymph nodes in the neck. This is because the body's immune system is trying to fight off the gum disease bacteria. The herbs turmeric and ginger can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has powerful anti-inflammatory properties, while ginger has antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections.  People with bleeding gums may also experience headaches, especially if the infection has spread to the sinuses. The herb feverfew can help to reduce inflammation and ease headaches. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.  Some people with bleeding gums may experience digestive issues, such as diarrhea or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The herb slippery elm can help to soothe and protect the digestive tract. Slippery elm contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues.  Finally, people with bleeding gums may experience fatigue and weakness. The herb ashwagandha can help to boost energy levels and reduce stress. Ashwagandha contains adaptogenic compounds that help to balance the body's energy and reduce fatigue.  These medicinal herbs can help to alleviate these symptoms because they contain compounds that have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to fight off infections, reduce inflammation, and ease symptoms, allowing the body to heal and recover from gum disease and related health issues.
